coaching.title: Easy 8mi Hilly — Build Phase
coaching.message: Calendar has you down for 8 miles on hilly terrain, ~1,500 ft of vert, all in Zone 2 (125-150 bpm, comfortably conversational). This is aerobic-building work, not a hammer session — if your HR drifts toward 160 on the climbs, hike them. Antigua sits at ~1,500m, so altitude will nudge your HR up a few beats; honor the zone by effort, not pace. Treat every climb as Julian Alps rehearsal: short, efficient steps, hands on quads, eyes up. You're 13 weeks out and squarely in Build — consistency over heroics today.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: Eat a carb-forward breakfast 60-90 min before (your bananas/fruit/oats plan is perfect — aim ~60-80g carbs pre-run). At 8 miles you're near the threshold where intra-run fuel matters; if you'll be out past 75 min, take 30-40g carbs mid-run plus fluid with electrolytes — Antigua's heat and elevation accelerate sweat loss. Within 30 min of finishing, hit your recovery window: ~25-30g protein (tofu works) plus carbs to refill glycogen. Train the gut now so race-day fueling is automatic.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: After an easy hilly effort, prioritize legs-elevated time and 10 minutes of calf, quad, and glute mobility — the descents load eccentrically even at easy pace. Hydrate aggressively given the altitude. You've got a full social day (dinner, chocolate making) so bank an early-ish wind-down and protect 7-8 hours of sleep tonight; aerobic adaptation is cemented in deep sleep, not on the trail.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: Build Phase is where the race is quietly won — no single run feels heroic, but stacked weeks of honest Zone 2 are exactly what carries you up 2,500m of alpine vert in September. Today, practice patience: let easy be easy. The discipline to hold back now is the same discipline that keeps you composed at mile 25 in the Julian Alps. Steady is fast.
